title: R-Services Exposed (rexec/rlogin/rsh - Ports 512-514)
description: Legacy Berkeley R-services (rexecd on port 512, rlogind on port 513, rsh on port 514) are running. These services use weak trust-based authentication (.rhosts files) and transmit credentials in cleartext. They can be exploited for unauthorized remote command execution if trust relationships exist.
evidence:
Service enumeration confirmed:
- Port 512/tcp: exec (netkit-rsh rexecd) - open
- Port 513/tcp: login (OpenBSD or Solaris rlogind) - open
- Port 514/tcp: tcpwrapped - open

These services rely on .rhosts trust files and IP-based authentication, which are trivially spoofable. All communication is unencrypted.
remediation: Disable all R-services (rexecd, rlogind, rsh). Use SSH for all remote administration. Remove any .rhosts and hosts.equiv trust files.

issues: HIGH severity finding should have stronger evidence
Exploitation proof required for high/critical findings
suggestions: Add command output demonstrating access or data extraction
For high findings, demonstrate actual impact
Actually exploit the vulnerability and capture output
